mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice paths for bootstrapping a secure and functional cluster. While it is user-friendly, it requires a good understanding of Kubernetes concepts for effective use.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00

```

> Add Google Cloud's public key for package verification.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list to include the new repository.
> Install specific versions of kubeadm, kubectl, and kubelet.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command initializes the Kubernetes control plane on the master node, setting up the cluster.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=10.244.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the new cluster by setting up the kubeconfig file.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the .kube directory for configuration.
> Copy the admin.conf file to the user's kubeconfig.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coreos/flannel/master/Documentation/k8s-manifests/kube-flannel.yml

```

> Install Flannel as the pod network add-on.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their status.

uninstall.title: Reset Kubeadm Configuration
uninstall.description:
This command resets the kubeadm configuration and removes all Kubernetes components. Data loss may occur.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf /etc/kubernetes

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove Kubernetes configuration files.
> Remove the kubeconfig file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide > nodes-backup.txt

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node configuration.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be running or properly configured.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl status kubelet' to check the status and 'sudo systemctl restart kubelet' to restart it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to network issues or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the system meets the resource requirements and that the network is properly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the node is not fully initialized or the network add-on is not installed.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the network add-on and ensure it is correctly installed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the kubeconfig file is in place and has the correct permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features such as the PodSecurity admission controller and improved handling of network policies.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the --pod-network-cidr flag for kubeadm init.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that the network add-on is compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes showing 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
